issue_title: [OID4VCI] CredentialSignerException: Proof Type null is not supported for format ldp_vc
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

```
2025-12-12 12:10:37,447 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-58) Uncaught server error: org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.signing.CredentialSignerException: Proof Type null is not supported.
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.signing.LDCredentialSigner.getLinkedDataCryptographicSuite(LDCredentialSigner.java:80)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.signing.LDCredentialSigner.addProof(LDCredentialSigner.java:90)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.signing.LDCredentialSigner.signCredential(LDCredentialSigner.java:66)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.signing.LDCredentialSigner.signCredential(LDCredentialSigner.java:44)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.lambda$getCredential$10(OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java:1315)
	at java.base/java.util.Optional.map(Optional.java:260)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.getCredential(OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java:1315)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.requestCredential(OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java:802)

```

### Version

latest

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

get a valid ld credential

### Actual behavior

see above

### How to Reproduce?

accept an offer like this

```
{
    "credential_issuer": "http://localhost:8080/realms/oid4vci",
    "credential_configuration_ids": [
        "oid4vc_natural_person_ld"
    ],
    "grants": {
        "urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:pre-authorized_code": {
            "pre-authorized_code": "urn:oid4vci:code:BsiyQ3jKhWe2KYkgi1mH2WTvnWeACD8HtMMfckrAVRi40hfOh12GDPv2NBEtgnrf"
        }
    }
}
```

also fails for the non-pre-auth case

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
[OID4VCI] Disable ldp_vc format and providers to focus on JWT VC and SD-JWT VC (#46485)

closes #44875

Signed-off-by: forkimenjeckayang <forkimenjeckayang@gmail.com>

issue_title: Email is not updated based on upstream IdP email
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area identity-brokering ### Describe the bug When I login using an Identity Provider, I fill the email field in the first login flow form (IDP_REVIEW). After being authenticated, the email has not been persisted in the user profile. ### Version 26.3.0 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior All fields in the IDP_REVIEW form must be persisted in the user profile. ### Actual behavior The email field has not been updated on the user profile. UPDATED_EMAIL event has been emitted in User events tab. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Configure a Idp with first login flow. 2. Create a user Bob in KC. 3. Login with the user Bob from the Idp (i'm using another Keycloak instance as Idp) 4. On the IDP_REVIEW form, fill user profile informations (including email) 5. After IDP_REVIEW confirmation and successfull authentication redirection, go to Bob's user profile. The email attribute is still empty, it has not been updated with the value provided in the email field of the IDP_REVIEW form. ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
Update email when linking account when sync mode is FORCE

Closes #44905

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Run on server with remote uses old classes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

When running tests that uses run-on-server with the remote server classes are cached until server is restarted. If the run-on-server statements are changed in the test and ran again the previously cached classes are used. 

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

For each execution of tests the classes loaded on the server side should be re-loaded

### Actual behavior

Classes are only loaded once on the server side and never invalidated

### How to Reproduce?

Run a test that uses run-on-server with the remote server, then change the statements for example add a System.out.println and observe that it doesn't change until the server is restarted

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Clear classes loaded on the server side for run-on-server when a new execution happens (#44909)

Closes #44908

Signed-off-by: stianst <stianst@gmail.com>

issue_title: JWTClientSecretAuthenticator throws NPE if client assertion is not set
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

If `JWTClientSecretAuthenticator` is invoked when the client assertion is not set it throws a NPE

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Skips the authenticator if no client assertion is set

### Actual behavior

Throws NPE

### How to Reproduce?

Try authenticating a client with X509 and an error will be shown in the server logs

### Anything else?

Example from server log:
```
2025-12-16 12:05:51,156 ERROR [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-1) KC-SERVICES0025: Error when validating client assertion: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.representations.JsonWebToken.getIssuer()" because "jwt" is null
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.client.JWTClientSecretAuthenticator.authenticateClient(JWTClientSecretAuthenticator.java:63)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.ClientAuthenticationFlow.processFlow(ClientAuthenticationFlow.java:74)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationProcessor.authenticateClient(AuthenticationProcessor.java:965)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.AuthorizeClientUtil.authorizeClient(AuthorizeClientUtil.java:51)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint.checkClient(TokenEndpoint.java:180)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint.processGrantRequest(TokenEndpoint.java:136)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint$quarkusrestinvoker$processGrantRequest_3903cccf0670c489ab77dc2ba1ba757573ec6d78.invoke(Unknown Source)
```
commit_message:
Fix NPE in JWT authenticators (#44941)

Closes #44940

Signed-off-by: stianst <stianst@gmail.com>

issue_title: Test framework supplier dependencies broken
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

Currently a supplier gets a dependency from the registry while it is being created. This works fine as long as the dependency is requested by the test, but if it is not then the registry will just look up whatever exists in the registry. This can lead to a non-compatible dependency being used.

An example of this is found in ManagedCertificates and AbstractKeycloakServerSupplier. If one test runs with a custom ManagedCertificates configuration, this is left running after the test. Now when the AbstractKeycloakServerSupplier looks up the dependency it just re-uses this instance, instead of a new one with the default config.

A workaround was introduced while adding mTLS support to ManagedCertificates, but this is only for the above situation. In general dependencies must be declared upfront by suppliers so the registry can check for compatibility. This requires changing how dependencies are retrieved by all suppliers, which was out of scope for that PR.

This issue is a follow-up to update all suppliers to declare dependencies up-front, and refactor registry to not allow getting dependencies that have not been declared already.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Dependencies are re-created as needed

### Actual behavior

Can attach to non-compatible dependency

### How to Reproduce?

Remove getDependencies implementation in AbstractKeycloakServerSupplier

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Update testframework registry to explicitly declare dependencies in all suppliers (#44974)

Closes #44947, Closes #40756

Signed-off-by: stianst <stianst@gmail.com>

issue_title: Authorization_details added to token-response even when should not be
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Consider this scenario:

- OIDC Authentication login request sent with some scope corresponding to OID4VCI (EG. `scope=oid4vc_natural_person`) and with the `authorization_details` parameter asking for creating credential-offer of some OID4VCI credential
- Credential-offer is created and `authorization_details` are added to the token response -> OK
- Now assume that there is 2nd OIDC authentication login request to the same client in the same browser. This is normal SSO login request, which does not do anything related to OID4VCI . However in the token-response, the `authorization_details` are still returned after this 2nd OIDC authentication flow is finished  -> KO
- Also I think that the access-token returned from this 2nd-request is possible to be used in the credential-request as that 2nd request created OID4VCI credential-offer -> KO

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-17)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only the 1st token-response, which was triggered from the OIDC flow asking for OID4VCI credential, is supposed to return `authorization_details` and create credential-offer. The subsequent OIDC login requests in same browser session should not do that (especially if those requests are regular SSO login requests not asking for any OID4VCI scope or authorization_details)

### Actual behavior

The subsequent token-responses (2nd and others) are adding `authorization_details` even if they should not

### How to Reproduce?

See details

### Anything else?

I think we can add `authorization_details` as a claim into access-token (and maybe also probably refresh-token if issued) instead of adding them as a note in userSession. Storing them inside token instead of userSession can make sure that credential-request can be successful just if the sent token contains the relevant `authorization_details` . That should also help with fixing the issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/44670 and probably also https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/44534 as userSession may not be needed anymore when pre-authorization flow is used.
commit_message:
Polishing based on PR review. Fix flaky tests
closes #44961

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: Missing message keys for USER_SESSION_DELETED
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug The Admin UI message bundle https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/741c0ad959c9fe0daddcfab693fab5f378cd0d4b/js/apps/admin-ui/maven-resources/theme/keycloak.v2/admin/messages/messages_en.properties contains entries for all event types and `eventTypes.IMPERSONATE.name` and `eventTypes.IMPERSONATE.description` which are needed to provide context for an administrator. The entries for `USER_SESSION_DELETED` (together with the error entry) and potentially others are missing. ### Version main ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Each user event should have two entries in the message resource bundle ### Actual behavior The key is missing ### How to Reproduce? Look at the file and the admin UI ### Anything else? Please provide a unit test that checks this so that future problems do not show up. This might be a Java test in `server-spi-private` as a change there should possibly trigger the error. Or a JavaScript based test in the admin-UI that owns the translations.
commit_message:
Missing message keys for USER_SESSION_DELETED

Closes #44966

issue_title: tools commands accepts any arguments
issue_body:
### Description

For example `./kc.sh tools windows-service --foo=dev-mem` does not produce an error. The command should actually not accept any property mapper / spi arguments, let alone someing completely invalid like --foo.

See  https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/44496#discussion_r2627649298

tools completion is a special case since it is derived from a help command such that no args are considered unrecognized.
commit_message:
fix: preventing args to service commands (#45050)

also outputting all commands for help files

closes #44975

Signed-off-by: Steve Hawkins <shawkins@redhat.com>

issue_title: Deadlock in Infinispan virtual threads
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

As reported in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/43508#issuecomment-3670695086 there can be a deadlock when there is a state transfer

### Version

26.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

There should be no deadlocks

### Actual behavior

There are deadlocks. 

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/43508#issuecomment-3671935155 and https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/43508#issuecomment-3672199467 for the thread dumps and logs.

### How to Reproduce?

Issue frequent state transfers

### Anything else?

I'll attempt to create a workaround

issue_title: Enable HelpCommandDistTest on Windows runners
issue_body:
### Description

This test was historically run only on Linux. With the introduction of the Windows service, the command tree now differs, so enabling the test is justified.

An initial attempt was made as part of the Windows service PR; however, enabling the test exposed an issue where the approved and received command outputs did not match, most likely due to whitespace differences. The original analysis is available [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/44496#discussion_r2632832457).
commit_message:
Enable HelpCommandDistTest on Windows runners (#45197)

* Enable HelpCommandDistTest on Windows runners

Closes: #45044

issue_title: Default redirect URI for an organization should be the Home URL of the account client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

Follow up of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/33201

When an organization’s redirect URL is left empty, Keycloak currently defaults to the account console URL. This ignores the `account` client’s configured Home URL (base URL). As a result, installations that customize the `account` client base URL will make users redirected to the wrong location after organization invitation is completed.

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If the organization redirect URL is blank, Keycloak should resolve and use the account client’s base URL (including root URL resolution), and only fall back to the account console URL if the account client is missing or has no base URL set.

### Actual behavior

`account` client’s configured Home URL (base URL) is ignored and redirected to the wrong location after organization registration or invitation acceptance.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an organization and leave the redirect URL blank.
2. Send an Invitation to the user for the organization.
3. The user completes the Invitation process and then redirected to the account console even if `account` client's Home URL is customized

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix organization invitation redirect to respect account client base URL

When an organization's redirect URL is left empty, Keycloak currently defaults
to the account console URL, ignoring the account client's configured Home URL
(base URL). This fix checks the account client's base URL before falling back
to the default account console URL.

Changes:
- Added resolveAccountClientBaseUrl() helper method in OrganizationInvitationResource
- Added setBaseUrl() method to ClientAttributeUpdater test utility
- Added integration tests for the new behavior

Closes #45052

Signed-off-by: Rathan Naik <30756840+Rathan-Naik@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Base theme, config-totp: `label` wrapped in a `kcInputWrapperClass` class
issue_body:
Hello,

apologies if this is a duplicate, at the best of my knowledge it isn't

Are the following:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login-config-totp.ftl#L55

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login-config-totp.ftl#L77

supposed to be `kcLabelWrapperClass` instead?
commit_message:
Fix label wrapper class in config-totp template

The labels in `login-config-totp.ftl` were wrapped with the
`kcInputWrapperClass` instead of the intended `kcLabelWrapperClass`.

This caused incorrect styling in custom themes inheriting the base
theme.

Update the markup to use `kcLabelWrapperClass`, matching the rest of the
theme and ensuring consistent label styling.

Closes #45053

Signed-off-by: Geremia Taglialatela <tagliala.dev@gmail.com>

issue_title: Base theme: "user properties" and "register" required mark is missing the required class
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area login/ui ### Describe the bug Hello, apologies if this is a duplicate, at the best of my knowledge it isn't. I've also used Copilot to attempt to find duplicates Is the following: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/user-profile-commons.ftl#L45 https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/register.ftl#L20 https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/register.ftl#L47 Supposed to be ```diff - <#if attribute.required>* + <#if attribute.required>* ``` ? ### Version `main` branch ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior I was expecting that all required chars were wrapped in a `` tag ### Actual behavior The `*` is being added as text to the page ### How to Reproduce? https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/user-profile-commons.ftl#L45 https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/register.ftl#L20 https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/register.ftl#L47 ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
Wrap required indicators with "required" span

Some asterisks indicating required fields in `user-profile-commons.ftl`
and `register.ftl` are not wrapped in a `<span class="required">` tag.

This caused incorrect styling in custom themes that inherit the base theme.

Update the markup to wrap required field indicators with
`<span class="required">`, matching the rest of the theme and ensuring
consistent styling.

Closes #45058

Signed-off-by: Geremia Taglialatela <tagliala.dev@gmail.com>

issue_title: Client deletion timeout due to large number of client roles
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

We are seeing increased timeouts since upgrading from 25.0.6 to 26.4.0 when calling delete on clients that have ~30 client roles attached. This is happening only in an environment with a large number of clients and users, so it seems the latency increase scales with database size: pushing latencies over the timeout window for large environments. We are seeing large numbers of DB queries issued from this operation, with pretty substantial time gaps between some of them (probably ispn syncing across our distributed nodes, though we haven't gotten trace data for that).

We tracked down the source of the issue to the [removeRole](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/1e48f9e512feb4795fdbba23910b3288692e64e7/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/JpaRealmProvider.java#L456) method, which changed in the upgrade.

### Version

26.4.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Client deletion does not timeout in large environments

### Actual behavior

Client deletion times out in large environments

### How to Reproduce?

Given a telemetry agent (like datadog) is connected:
1. Run keycloak version 25
2. Delete a client
3. Run keycloak version 26
4. Delete an identical client
5. Observe a larger number of DB queries in version 26


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Optimize deletion of composite roles

Closes #45065

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <alexander.schwartz@ibm.com>

Hello,

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login-config-totp.ftl#L56

commit_message:
Fix label class in config-totp template

The labels in `login-config-totp.ftl` were statically styled with a
`form-control` class instead of the intended dynamic `kcLabelClass `
property.

This caused incorrect styling in custom themes inheriting the base
theme.

Update the markup to use `kcLabelClass`, matching the rest of the theme
and ensuring consistent label styling.

issue_title: ServiceMonitor is not created by keycloak operator
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

When creating a keycloak instance, no service-monitor is created although explicitly enabled (even though this is default anyway, and the documentation only describes how to disable it).

Digging into the code, I realized that the service monitor will only be created once `metrics-enabled` is configured as keycloak option.

### Version

v26.4.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

After applying the information from the documentation, the service monitor is created.

### Actual behavior

Service monitor not created due to missing documentation.

### How to Reproduce?

Install keycloak through operator without setting `metrics-enabled`. Check prometheus.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Extend documentation on ServiceMonitor creation (#45071)

The documentation so far only mentions how to disable the ServiceMonitor, leading to the assumption that it is enabled by default.

Getting a service monitor created also requires enabling metrics in keycloak, though:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/658faf210f2ff91ee254ff9fcc62ddfbcbcd9e42/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/controllers/KeycloakServiceMonitorDependentResource.java#L50

While the missing setting creates a warning, the administrator should already have this information before applying the configuration.

Closes #45070

Signed-off-by: Jens Erat <email@jenserat.de>

issue_title: Workflows documentation has non-working workflow examples
issue_body:

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

The workflows documentation has a few workflow examples in YAML that are incorrect - i.e. if you copy and paste the example when creating a new workflow, the creation will fail due to errors.

- User onboarding example in `Understanding common use cases` is referencing the `assign-user-role` step, which doesn't exist. It should be `grant-role`.

### Version

nightly

### Regression

Examples in the documentation should work when creating new workflows

### Actual behavior

Workflow creation fails due to not-existing providers.

### How to Reproduce?

Copy the aforementioned examples, then go to the Admin UI -> Workflows -> Create workflow. Paste the example there and hit `Create`. Error message is displayed

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Enable visibility of Role Mapping tab for users with view-users role
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Currently "Role Mapping" tab in the group section is only visible to users who have "manage-users".
The Admin API already allows viewing these mappings with the view-users role, so the UI should be updated to reflect this capability.
This was only a frontend change.

### Version

26.4.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Users with the view-users role should be able to see and click on the "role mapping" tab but not update role-mappings.

### Actual behavior

The "Role Mapping" tab is completely hidden for any user who does not have the manage-users role, even if they have view-users.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new user
2. Give the user query-groups and view-users role.
3. Log in as the user in the admin console
4. Go to groups, click on a group.
5. Role tabbing is not visible.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Allow view-users role to see Groups->Role Mapping tab.

Fixes #45093

issue_title: CVE-2025-66021 in Keycloak Services
issue_body:
### Description

There is a security concern identified in the Keycloak base package:

- **Affected Component:** org.keycloak:keycloak-services:26.4.0
- **Transitive Dependency:** com.googlecode.owasp-java-html-sanitizer:owasp-java-html-sanitizer:20240325.1
- **CVE:** CVE-2025-66021
- **Issue:** Vulnerability related to XSS via HtmlPolicyBuilder when using noscript and style tags.

When can we expect a fix for this?

issue_title: Failed upgrade to 26.4.7 - sql generated for manual database upgrade contains invalid statements
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Upgrading database from 26.3.4 -> 26.4.7 failed.
We are using manual strategy for upgrades (`--spi-connections-jpa--quarkus--migration-strategy=manual`).
SQL generated for upgrades to version 26.4.x is incorrect. It contains:
```SQL
-- Changeset META-INF/jpa-changelog-26.4.0.xml::26.4.0-40933-saml-encryption-attributes::keycloak
-- WARNING The following SQL may change each run and therefore is possibly incorrect and/or invalid:
INSERT INTO KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT_ATTRIBUTES (CLIENT_ID,NAME,VALUE) SELECT ID, ?, ? FROM KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT WHERE PROTOCOL = ? AND ID NOT IN (SELECT CLIENT_ID FROM KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT_ATTRIBUTES WHERE NAME = ?);

INSERT INTO KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT_ATTRIBUTES (CLIENT_ID,NAME,VALUE) SELECT ID, ?, ? FROM KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT WHERE PROTOCOL = ? AND ID NOT IN (SELECT CLIENT_ID FROM KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT_ATTRIBUTES WHERE NAME = ?);

INSERT INTO KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT_ATTRIBUTES (CLIENT_ID,NAME,VALUE) SELECT ID, ?, ? FROM KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT WHERE PROTOCOL = ? AND ID NOT IN (SELECT CLIENT_ID FROM KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC.CLIENT_ATTRIBUTES WHERE NAME = ?);
```
Which contains parametrized sql's without values.

We are using Oracle, using: `ojdbc17-23.6.0.24.10.jar` and `orai18n-23.6.0.24.10.jar`

### Version

26.4.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

SQL's for DB upgrade with manual strategy contains proper statements. 

### Actual behavior

SQL's for DB upgrade with manual strategy contains invalid SQL statements.

### How to Reproduce?

Try to generate DB upgrade SQL for database with version 26.3.4 to 26.4.7 (probably every 26.4.x version).
```
/.../keycloak-26.4.7/bin/kc.sh \
    start \
    --spi-connections-jpa-quarkus-migration-strategy=manual \
    --spi-connections-jpa-quarkus-initialize-empty=false \
    --spi-connections-jpa-quarkus-migration-export="$SQL_PATH"
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Use RawSqlStatement instead of RawParameterizedSqlStatement for Saml encryption update
Closes #45107

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Admin UI: User attribute filter reset does not refresh user list
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Clicking "Reset" in the user attribute search form clears the filter state but does not refresh the user list. The
   previous search results remain displayed.

  This is confusing when searching for a non-existent value returns empty results - after reset, users think there's
   no data.

### Version

latest

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Reset should clear filters and refresh the user list to show all users.

### Actual behavior

Reset clears filters internally but the user list stays unchanged

### How to Reproduce?

1. Go to Admin Console → Users
2. Switch to "Search by user attribute"
3. Search with a non-existent value (e.g., username = "non-exist-user")
4. Click "Reset"
5. User list still shows empty instead of all users

### Video:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b88e8978-c678-4f25-91d7-bb05e4e0ad04

### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #45108

issue_title: Realm-level admininistrators can no longer use Admin Console since 26.3.0 (UI fails to render)
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug Starting with Keycloak 26.3.0, a realm-level administrator (user with the `realm-admin` role in a non-master realm) can no longer use the realm-specific Admin Console. After authenticating directly against **the realm-level console URL** (e.g. /admin/_\_/console/#/), the UI becomes largely unusable: most components do not render correctly and the web console is effectively non-functional. This workflow worked as expected in 26.2.5 and earlier versions: realm-level administrators were able to access the realm-specific admin console to perform maintenance tasks. From 26.3.0 through 26.4.7 (latest at time of reporting), realm-level administration appears to be possible only by signing into the master realm Admin Console with a server administrator account and switching to the target realm, which is a functional regression for delegated realm administration. ### Version 26.4.7 ### Regression - [x] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior A user with realm-level administrative permissions (e.g., `realm-admin` in realm Test) should be able to: 1. Navigate to the realm-specific Admin Console endpoint: http://localhost:8080/admin/Test/console/#/ 2. Authenticate successfully 3. Use a fully rendered, functional Admin Console UI for that realm (as in 26.2.5 and earlier) ### Actual behavior After logging in to the realm-level Admin Console (/admin/_\_/console/#/) as the realm admin user: - The Admin Console UI is broken / mostly not rendered - Navigation and UI components are not usable - The console cannot be used to perform administrative tasks from the realm-level entry point ### How to Reproduce? 1. Install Keycloak 26.3.0+ 2. Create a realm named `Test` 3. Create a new user in realm `Test` and grant them the `realm-admin` role 4. Open the realm-level Admin Console URL, for example: http://localhost:8080/admin/Test/console/#/ 6. Log in as the realm-level administrator created in step 3 **Observe:** the Admin Console UI does not render correctly and is not usable. ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
Handle fragment when accessing the admin console

Closes #45116

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Missing icons in v2 keycloak login theme
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area login/ui ### Describe the bug Some icons are missing with the v2 login theme. e.g. in the select-authenticator template or the webauthn registration. ### Version 26.4.7 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior with the v1 theme: notice the list and key icons. notice the key icons. ### Actual behavior with the v2 theme: ### How to Reproduce? Switch to v2 theme. ### Anything else? Other missing icons I noticed are in the login screen's "alert info box": from `template.ftl`: ``` <#-- App-initiated actions should not see warning messages about the need to complete the action --> <#-- during login. --> <#if displayMessage && message?has_content && (message.type != 'warning' || !isAppInitiatedAction??)>
<#if message.type = 'success'> <#if message.type = 'warning'> <#if message.type = 'error'> <#if message.type = 'info'>
${kcSanitize(message.summary)?no_esc}
``` (not sure how to get this to show up with an unmodified theme) I think the problem is caused by these class definitions being referenced by the v2 theme templates but not defined in its `theme.properties` file: ``` kcFeedbackErrorIcon kcFeedbackInfoIcon kcFeedbackSuccessIcon kcFeedbackWarningIcon kcLabelWrapperClass kcSelectAuthListItemArrowIconClass kcSelectAuthListItemIconPropertyClass kcWebAuthnKeyIcon ``` copying the definitions of the v1 theme's `theme.properties` fixes the issue for me for all but `kcWebAuthnKeyIcon`, which I had to set to `fa fa-key` to get to work. Note that there are some non-icon related class definitions that were defined in v1 theme but not in v2 though they are still referenced by the v2 theme: ``` kcButtonDefaultClass kcButtonLargeClass kcCheckInputClass kcFormButtonsClass kcFormOptionsClass kcFormSocialAccountSectionClass kcInputHelperTextAfterClass kcInputHelperTextBeforeClass kcInputWrapperClass ```
commit_message:
Add missing icons for keycloak.v2 login theme
Closes #45162

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Base theme: `login-config-totp`, buttons are not wrapped in a `kcFormGroupClass`
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

Usually buttons are wrapped in a `<div class="${properties.kcFormGroupClass!}">`

Examples:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login-update-password.ftl#L62-L73

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login-password.ftl#L43-L45

This is not the case for config-totp:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login-config-totp.ftl#L98-L112


### Version

main branch

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Buttons to be wrapped in a div with `kcFormGroupClass` class


### Actual behavior

buttons are not wrapped, causing `margin` inconsistencies across template

### How to Reproduce?

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e6b2f0dc60d349560569fac7f775d27e2efd83c1/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login-config-totp.ftl#L98-L112


### Anything else?

As a workaround, I've extracted the template and customized
commit_message:
Wrap form buttons in `kcFormButtonsClass` div

Previously, the buttons in `login-config-totp.ftl` were not enclosed
in a `<div>` with the `${properties.kcFormButtonsClass!}` class.

This resulted in inconsistent margins and alignment compared to other
base theme templates, especially affecting button groups.

Update the markup to wrap the buttons in a `kc-form-buttons` div
with the appropriate class. This aligns the template with the styling
standards used elsewhere and ensures margin consistency across the UI.

Related to: #45164

Signed-off-by: Geremia Taglialatela <tagliala.dev@gmail.com>

issue_title: Ensure failed steps don't cancel the workflow execution
issue_body:
### Description

While testing the activation of workflows for a large number of resources, we've noticed that in some cases the first step would fail to run (like the notify step failing due to rate-limits of the mail server), causing the workflow to be dettached from the resource. We need to ensure the failure is properly logged, and the workflow remains active.
commit_message:
Add section about handling failures to the workflows documentation

Closes #45175

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: [OID4VCI] Issuer metadata contains unwanted 'id' for credential_configurations_supported
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Current credential metadata contains an unwanted 'id' property

```
   "oid4vc_natural_person": {
      "id": "oid4vc_natural_person",
      "format": "dc+sd-jwt",
      "scope": "oid4vc_natural_person",
      "cryptographic_binding_methods_supported": [
        "jwk"
      ],
      "credential_signing_alg_values_supported": [
        "PS384",
        "RS384",
        "EdDSA",
        "ES384",
        "ES256",
        "RS256",
        "ES512",
        "PS256",
        "PS512",
        "RS512"
      ],
      "vct": "oid4vc_natural_person",
      "credential_definition": {
        "type": [
          "oid4vc_natural_person"
        ],
        "@context": [
          "oid4vc_natural_person"
        ]
      },
      "proof_types_supported": {
        "attestation": {
          "proof_signing_alg_values_supported": [
            "PS384",
            "RS384",
            "EdDSA",
            "ES384",
            "ES256",
            "RS256",
            "ES512",
            "PS256",
            "PS512",
            "RS512"
          ]
        },
        "jwt": {
          "proof_signing_alg_values_supported": [
            "PS384",
            "RS384",
            "EdDSA",
            "ES384",
            "ES256",
            "RS256",
            "ES512",
            "PS256",
            "PS512",
            "RS512"
          ]
        }
      },
      "credential_metadata": {
        "claims": [
          {
            "path": [
              "id"
            ],
            "mandatory": false
          },
          {
            "path": [
              "email"
            ],
            "mandatory": false
          },
          {
            "path": [
              "firstName"
            ],
            "mandatory": false
          },
          {
            "path": [
              "familyName"
            ],
            "mandatory": false
          }
        ]
      }
    }
```

### Version

latest

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The configuration id is already given by the key in the map.
Scope and credential type can be defined different from that id (which is correct)

### Actual behavior

No explicit/duplicate 'id' property - wallets should be able to rely on map keys for credential_configurations_supported

### How to Reproduce?

check http://0.0.0.0:8080/.well-known/openid-credential-issuer/realms/master

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
[OID4VCI] Issuer metadata contains unwanted 'id' for credential_configurations_supported (#45210)

closes #45209

Signed-off-by: Thomas Diesler <tdiesler@ibm.com>

issue_title: User REST Admin API - count and search returns different amount of users
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Problem: via REST API, when I use `GET /admin/realms/{realm}/users/count?exact=true` endpoint, Keycloak returns all users except this service account user. But when I call `GET /admin/realms/{realm}/users?exact=true`, it returns the same list of users plus this service user as well.

The misalignment was already partially fixed in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/41980, but missed the `exact` check in the if statement of the count function (see below) 


### Version

26.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

`/users/count?exact=true` and `users?exact=true` behaviour should be aligned and return the same number of users

### Actual behavior

`users?exact=true` returns also the service account users, while the `count?exact=true` does not

### How to Reproduce?

- create a new realm
- create a client of type service account in the realm
- invoke the `users/count?exact=true`, the result is 0
- invoke the `users/count?exact=true`, it returns the service account (1 user)

### Anything else?

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/release/26.4/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UsersResource.java#L323 the `if` statements in the getUsers method contains an `|| exact != null`. While the same if statement in the getUsersCount does not https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/release/26.4/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UsersResource.java#L452.

It also seems that the not null condition on `idpAlias` and `idpUserId` are not aligned.
commit_message:
User REST Admin API - count and search returns different amount of users

Closes #45219

Signed-off-by: Martin Kanis <mkanis@redhat.com>
